seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Denhams Beach and Deniliquin. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Denhams Beach vs Deniliquin: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Denhams Beach and Deniliquin.
heading: Denhams Beach vs Deniliquin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Denhams Beach, New South Wales and Deniliquin, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Denhams Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Deniliquin (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Denhams Beach or Deniliquin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Denhams Beach has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Deniliquin scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Denhams Beach scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Deniliquin scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Denhams Beach or Deniliquin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Denhams Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
